---
title: "Principles of strength training"
sidebarTitle: "Principles"
description: "Your body does not want to build muscle. Here is how you make it believe it needs to."
icon: "atom"
---

Understand this. Your body loves to stay where it is. It does not want to build more muscle than it requires to survive. So if you don't have muscle right now, don't hate your body. It just doesn't believe you need it. It is conserving its resources.

So you make your body believe that you need more muscle. How do you do this? By using your muscles more than what they are capable of. By reaching the point where you have exhausted your capacity and you still want more.

That is what signals your body.

> Hey, Satya is using his muscles to his capacity and wants to use them more. I need to give him that muscle.

Enough analogies. In scientific terms, you need to break your muscles so that your body repairs them and makes them stronger so that they don't break again. And then you go break them again.

## Why the gym

Now you can do any kind of activity and push to your limits. You can climb trees, throw rocks in the sea, and if you do that enough to break your muscles, you will build muscle.

But that's not what you want. You want to build muscle in the fastest possible time, to look sexy in the fastest possible time.

So that is why we need a gym.

Now the question is how do you break them without injuring yourself.
